findings.message: Tautological assertion `assert s is s` — an object is always identical to itself, so this test passes regardless of any behaviour in `Settings`. It exercises nothing about construction, defaults, or invariants and cannot fail.
findings.suggestion: Assert a meaningful property of the constructed Settings, e.g. `assert Settings().service_name == "demo"` or `assert isinstance(Settings(), Settings) and Settings().environment == "dev"` — something tied to the code under test.

findings.message: Over-mocked / asserts on the mock itself: the test monkeypatches `Settings.resolved_log_level` to return the literal "MOCKED" and then asserts that `Settings().resolved_log_level == "MOCKED"`. The real `resolved_log_level` implementation is never executed, so this test passes no matter how `resolved_log_level` is (or isn't) implemented — it cannot fail in response to a real regression.
findings.suggestion: Drop the patch and drive the property through its real inputs (e.g. set `APP_LOG_LEVEL` via `monkeypatch.setenv` and assert the resolved value, including default and case-normalisation behaviour). Only mock collaborators, not the unit under test.
